pattern: Because doing the earliest-finishing ride of one category first is always optimal for that ordering, the whole search collapses to two linear scans instead of checking every pair.
hints: You take one ride from each category and pick which goes first; the second ride starts at the max of its opening time and your current finish.
For a fixed 'which category goes first', the best first ride is simply the one that finishes earliest.
Compute each category's earliest finish, then for each ordering scan the other category for the best second ride; the answer is the smaller of the two orderings.

edge_inputs.description: earliest-opening ride is not part of the best plan
many rides each, best uses a mid pair

stress.description: larger random ride sets to exercise the linear approach
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50000))
    m = max(1, min(size, 50000))
    ls = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    ld = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    ws = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(m)]
    wd = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(m)]
    return [ls, ld, ws, wd]
